sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":860655,"gaz_name":"Mott Canyon","gaz_featureclass":"Valley","fcode":15050,"state_alpha":"NV","county_name":"Douglas"} — geometry polyline (Web Mercator); downstream/mouth vertex (-13341016.1277, 4711681.4689) converts to lon -119.84439, lat 38.93022, which is ~298m from the file's recorded coordinate (-119.841, 38.9307); the other end (-13346264.881, 4712029.0966) converts to lon -119.89154, lat 38.93265, tracing the canyon upstream toward the Carson Range crest.

parameters.note: Mott Canyon Creek is a small perennial creek draining Mott Canyon on the Nevada (east) side of the Carson Range near Kingsbury/Heavenly, in Douglas County. NDOW's own water-body database lists it (Region: Western, County: Douglas, Type of Water: Creek or Stream) but it is not named in the Douglas County CLOSED WATERS list (Topaz Marina jetties, the Cave Rock boat-launch area, and Glenbrook Creek from Lake Tahoe to Highway 50), nor in Douglas County's separate 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing — Mott Canyon drains east toward Kingsbury Grade/Carson Valley, away from the Lake Tahoe basin, similar to the neighboring Daggett Creek drainage. It therefore falls under the Douglas County OPEN WATERS general regulation: 'All waters (Except as noted below) — Open year around', with the county's general limits and any-hour fishing. No source names any fish species or stocking history for this creek, so species and stocking are left empty.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas Co. — OPEN WATERS: All waters)
regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round under the Douglas County general open-waters regulation. Mott Canyon Creek is not among the county's closed waters or its separate Lake Tahoe/tributaries listing.
regulations.verbatimText: All waters. (Except as noted below)    Open year around

parameters.counting_rule: Douglas County general open-waters limit: daily 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye); possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye). The 5/10 shown here is the trout component of that combined structure.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas Co. — OPEN WATERS: All waters)
regulations.humanSummary: Douglas County general limits: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish daily (max 5 black bass, 5 walleye); possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, 30 warmwater game fish (max 10 black bass, 10 walleye).
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye    Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye

parameters.note: The Douglas County general open-waters regulation allows fishing 'Any hour of the day or night' — unlike the county's 'Lake Tahoe and tributaries' listing, which restricts hours. Mott Canyon Creek, draining east away from the Lake Tahoe basin, follows the any-hour general rule.

regulations.citation: NV CR 25-16 (Douglas Co. — OPEN WATERS: All waters)
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing allowed any hour of the day or night (Douglas County general open-waters rule; no time-of-day restriction).
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: Nevada requires a fishing license for residents and nonresidents 12 years of age or older; anglers under 12 need no license. Mott Canyon Creek is a Carson Range/Kingsbury drainage water, not part of Lake Tahoe, so no Lake Tahoe California/Nevada license reciprocity applies — a Nevada fishing license is required.

regulations.humanSummary: Nevada fishing license required for anglers 12+.
regulations.verbatimText: Any resident 12 years of age or older who fishes in Nevada is required to have a fishing license.
